title: strychnos puberula
intro: Strychnos puberula, a species of flowering plant in the family Loganiaceae, is native to tropical Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, primarily due to the presence of alkaloids, which have been shown to possess antimalarial, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Strychnos puberula include the treatment of fever, malaria, and other tropical diseases, as well as its use as a purgative and antispasmodic. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, or used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
